nameMeanings.name: Burton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from a placename that is very common in central and northern England. The derivation in most cases is from Old English burh ‘fort’ (see Burke ) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).

nameMeanings.name: Joyce
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Apparently from the Norman male name Josce (Middle English Josse), which in turn is from Jodocus, a Latinized form of a Breton name, Iodoc, meaning ‘lord’, borne by a 7th-century Breton saint. The name was in use in England among Breton followers of William the Conqueror. However, although this was fairly common as a male given name in the Middle Ages, it had virtually died out by the 14th century. There is evidence of its use as a girl's name from the 16th century onwards in parishes with strong Puritan links, which suggests that it may have been associated with the vocabulary word joy; see Joy . It was strongly revived in the 19th century under the influence of popular fiction. It is borne by characters in Mrs Henry Wood's East Lynne ( 1861 ) and Edna Lyall's In the Golden Days ( 1885 ). Modern use may well have been influenced also by the common Irish surname derived from the medieval Norman male name. See also Joss .

nameMeanings.name: Coly
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the Irish surname, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Cuidighthigh ‘descendant of Cuidightheach’ (originally a byname for a helpful person), or of Mac Óda ‘son of Óda’ (a personal name of uncertain origin). Use as a given name in the United States especially has been at least in part inspired by William Frederick Cody ( 1846–1917 ), better known as ‘Buffalo Bill’, the showman of the Wild West.

variants.name: Codie
meanings: Transferred use of the Irish surname, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Cuidighthigh ‘descendant of Cuidightheach’ (originally a byname for a helpful person), or of Mac Óda ‘son of Óda’ (a personal name of uncertain origin). Use as a given name in the United States especially has been at least in part inspired by William Frederick Cody ( 1846–1917 ), better known as ‘Buffalo Bill’, the showman of the Wild West.

Use as a given name in the United States especially has been at least in part inspired by William Frederick Cody ( 1846–1917 ), better known as ‘Buffalo Bill’, the showman of the Wild West.

Use as a given name in the United States especially has been at least in part inspired by William Frederick Cody ( 1846–1917 ), better known as ‘Buffalo Bill’, the showman of the Wild West.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When Joyce Coly Burton was born in 1463, in England, her father, Sir Robert Burton Knight of Longnor, was 44 and her mother, Christian Stapleton, was 41. She had at least 9 sons and 5 daughters with Rev John Barker. She was buried in Hopton Castle, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Joyce Coly Burton(이)가 1463년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Robert Burton Knight of Longnor(은)는 44세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Christian Stapleton(은)는 41세였다. 그녀는 Rev John Barker(와)과 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 홉턴 카스틀에 묻혔다.
